name: Fleischman Consumer Law Center
description_short: Bankruptcy attorney Jay Fleischman offers Chapter 7/13 filing services, student loan management, and debt collection defense from offices in Los Angeles and New York.
description_long:
Jay Fleischman opened his law firm in 1995 with the explicit mission to counteract what he viewed as predatory lending practices and financial system inequities. He identified that lenders keep consumers 'uninformed, powerless, and afraid' and positioned his practice to provide transparency, legal guidance, and advocacy instead. The firm operates from two locations—Los Angeles, CA and New York, NY—serving clients nationwide through remote consultations.

Fleischman Consumer Law Center provides four core service areas: (1) Student Loan Law, including federal repayment strategy analysis, Public Service Loan Forgiveness eligibility review, consolidation and refinancing guidance, and balance reduction planning; (2) Consumer Bankruptcy, handling both Chapter 7 (debt elimination with asset protection) and Chapter 13 (wage-earner repayment plans); (3) Debt Collection Defense, including collection lawsuit defense, settlement negotiation, and claims against collectors for violations; and (4) Credit Reporting, addressing credit report errors and enforcement of consumer rights under fair reporting laws.

The firm distinguishes itself through Jay Fleischman's personal brand and 29+ years of practice history. Rather than operating as a high-volume bankruptcy mill, the practice emphasizes individual consultation via Zoom meetings to assess each client's specific situation and provide a 'step-by-step roadmap.' The website features client testimonials (5/5 Google rating example provided) and educational content addressing common consumer debt scenarios. Marketing emphasizes empowerment and clear information over fear-based urgency tactics.

However, as a solo/small practice, capacity may be limited compared to larger bankruptcy firms. The website provides minimal detail on fee structures, turnaround timelines, or success metrics. While the educational content is substantive, the firm relies heavily on personal brand recognition rather than institutional reputation. Prospective clients should verify licensing, specific Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 expertise, and fee arrangements before engaging.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 29+ years of law practice (firm founded 1995) demonstrates stability and experience
Specializes in student loan management alongside bankruptcy, addressing overlapping debt issues
Offers free initial consultations via Zoom or phone to assess options before engagement
Provides specific student loan forgiveness planning (PSLF eligibility, income-driven repayment analysis)
Explicitly handles debt collection harassment and illegal creditor practices, not just bankruptcy filing
Maintains dual office locations (LA and NY) while accepting remote clients nationwide
Published educational content on credit reports, debt defense, and post-bankruptcy credit recovery
cons: Solo practitioner model may limit availability and capacity for complex cases requiring multiple attorneys
Website contains no published fee schedule, hourly rates, or cost transparency for bankruptcy services
No information provided on Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 success rates, average case timelines, or discharge outcomes
Limited detail on how firm handles urgent bankruptcy filings or imminent foreclosure/wage garnishment situations
Relies heavily on founder's personal brand rather than institutional credentials or firm-wide reputation
best_for: Individuals with federal student loan debt seeking integrated bankruptcy and loan management strategy
Consumers experiencing debt collection harassment or lawsuits who need both defense and broader debt resolution
West Coast and Northeast clients (LA/NY coverage areas) with preference for remote, personalized consultations
Debtors seeking educational empowerment and transparent explanation of options rather than high-pressure filing services
diagnosis: Best for individuals seeking bankruptcy relief who also carry federal student loan debt and value personalized legal guidance over high-volume processing. Primary caveat: as a solo practitioner without published fee information or detailed service timelines, prospective clients should conduct due diligence on licensing, fee structure, and capacity before retaining services.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and debt elimination
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and wage-earner repayment plans
Federal student loan repayment strategy analysis and planning
Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) eligibility review and application support
Student loan consolidation and refinancing guidance
Collection lawsuit defense and courtroom representation
Debt settlement negotiation with creditors and collectors
Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) claims against collectors for harassment
Credit report error disputes and correction
Asset and property protection planning in bankruptcy
Post-bankruptcy credit recovery and credit score rebuilding guidance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 228 Park Ave S #47620, New York, NY 10003
